summary: Strong-pi pulsed ODMR alignment screen for tracked image172647 c01.
scientific_rationale: c01 is a freshly tracked bright NV candidate with final_counts_kcps=41.984. A magnetic-field-aligned ms=+1 NV should show a clear strong-pi pulsed ODMR dip near the current-field resonance around 3.875 GHz; no clear usable resonance in 3.825-3.925 GHz rejects this candidate for aligned follow-up.
expected_evidence: Terminal bridge result with savedexperiment path
Readout-aware resonance/alignment review; no automatic bridge fit
success_criteria: Bridge execute completes with nonzero averages and saved data
Raw/readout-aware review shows a clear usable resonance in 3.825-3.925 GHz
Counts/tracking do not collapse below hard safety gate
stop_conditions: Track/count gate fails with zero averages; then review as drift/count-gate evidence before retry
Hardware/safety/code uncertainty or explicit human STOP

expected_signal_model.transition: ms=+1 near 3.875 GHz in current field setup
expected_signal_model.contrast_basis: current setup reference from NV_RESEARCH_MEMORY: mS=0 to mS=+1 PL contrast about 22%; strong pi should be an easily visible resonance if aligned transition lies in window
expected_signal_model.distinguishability: Expected resonant dip ~22% is roughly 98x the binomial shot-noise floor and spans ~4.6 frequency steps, so a real aligned strong-pi resonance should be visible in raw/readout-aware review despite drift/noise.
